intro: The clearest split between Mapbox and OpenCage is vendor lock-in: Mapbox is rated medium (Tiles and nav proprietary), while OpenCage is rated high (OSM-based, easy to swap). Mapbox is Location platform with vector map tiles, navigation SDK, geocoding, and ML-powered maps; it fits best for apps. OpenCage is Geocoding API aggregating OpenStreetMap and other sources with batch geocoding and GDPR compliance; it fits best for apps. Pick Mapbox for apps; pick OpenCage for apps. Mapbox is not for simple projects. OpenCage is not for simple projects. Honest trade-offs: Mapbox is weak for apps without location features, and OpenCage is weak for apps without location features — match to your real scenario. Both sit under the Maps / Geolocation / Routing umbrella, so your final call depends on pricing transparency, integration effort, and which listed not-for situations you can actually tolerate. On pricing, Mapbox uses subscription pricing starting around $500/mo and OpenCage uses subscription pricing starting around 0.15, which materially changes total cost at scale.
quick_take: Mapbox is for apps; OpenCage is for apps; decide based on lock-in tolerance.
verdict_a: Choose Mapbox if medium lock-in (Tiles and nav proprietary) is acceptable and you need apps.
verdict_b: Choose OpenCage if high lock-in (OSM-based, easy to swap) is acceptable and you need apps.

faq.a: A direct comparison depends on your usage profile and which pricing model fits better. Mapbox uses subscription pricing starting at $500/mo. OpenCage uses subscription pricing starting at 0.15.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Mapbox to OpenCage?
faq.a: Migration may be non-trivial: OpenCage is rated high lock-in (OSM-based, easy to swap). Plan for data export, re-integration of SDKs, and re-testing flows before cutover. Verify exportable formats and feature parity against your live use cases before committing.

Mapbox scores higher on developer experience (5/5 vs 4/5 for OpenCage). Evaluate against your own stack: read their docs, run a quick SDK spike, and check error handling. Self-assessing with a small prototype beats trusting any rating at face value.

faq.q: Is OpenCage a good alternative to Mapbox?
faq.a: OpenCage sits in the same category as Mapbox, so it can be a reasonable alternative for overlapping use cases. OpenCage is best fit for apps. Note: OpenCage is not for simple projects.
